options.description: write a CPU profile to path
do not make any modifications to the destination directory
keep going as far as possible after an error
do not attempt to get a TTY for reading passwords
write output to path instead of stdout

subcommands.name: manage
subcommands.description: add an existing file, directory, or symlink to the source state

options.description: generate the template when adding files as templates
add files that should exist, irrespective of their contents
add symlink targets instead of symlinks
add symlinks with target in source or home dirs as templates

subcommands.name: apply
subcommands.description: update the destination directory to match the target state

subcommands.name: archive
subcommands.description: generate a tar archive of the target state

subcommands.name: cat
subcommands.description: print the target contents of a file, script, or symlink

subcommands.name: cd
subcommands.description: launch a shell in the source directory

subcommands.name: chattr
subcommands.description: change the attributes of a target in the source state

subcommands.name: completion
subcommands.description: generate shell completion code

subcommands.name: data
subcommands.description: print the template data

subcommands.name: decrypt
subcommands.description: decrypt file or standard input

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: print the diff between the target state and the destination state

options.description: reverse the direction of the diff

subcommands.name: doctor
subcommands.description: check your system for potential problems

subcommands.name: dump
subcommands.description: generate a dump of the target state

subcommands.name: edit
subcommands.description: edit the source state of a target

options.description: invoke editor with a hardlink to the source file

subcommands.name: edit-config
subcommands.description: edit the configuration file

subcommands.name: encrypt
subcommands.description: encrypt file or standard input

subcommands.name: execute-template
subcommands.description: execute the given template(s)

subcommands.name: unmanage
subcommands.description: remove a target from the source state

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: generate a file for use with chezmoi

subcommands.name: git
subcommands.description: run git in the source directory

subcommands.name: ignored
subcommands.description: print ignored targets

subcommands.name: import
subcommands.description: import an archive into the source state

options.description: set exact_ attribute on imported directories

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: setup the source directory and update the destination directory to match the target state

options.description: path to write generated config file
force prompt*Once template functions to prompt
purge config and source directories after running
use ssh instead of https when guessing dotfile repo URL

subcommands.name: license
subcommands.description: print license

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: list the managed entries in the destination directory

subcommands.name: merge
subcommands.description: perform a three-way merge between the destination state, the source state, and the target state

subcommands.name: merge-all
subcommands.description: perform a three-way merge for each modified file

subcommands.name: purge
subcommands.description: purge chezmoi's configuration and data

subcommands.name: re-add
subcommands.description: re-add modified files

subcommands.name: rm
subcommands.description: remove a target from the source state and the destination directory
